model_outputs.answer: The courses in the 5th semester have varying credits, evaluation patterns, and major topics. The course "Making Causal Inferences to Support Decisions" (CSE3025) has 3 credits (2L-0T-2P), with a final grade based on the marks obtained in the quiz, assignments, project, and other assessments, and the major topics include making causal inferences to support decisions. This information is supported by the course handout "Making Causal Inferences CSE3025 AY 2025-26 6th Sem.pdf" and "Course Handout Making Causal Inferences CSE3025 AY 2025-26 6th Sem.pdf". The course "Theory of Computation" (CSE2721) has 3 credits, with a final grade based on the marks obtained in the Mid-semester exam and other assessments, and the major topics include theory of computation. This information is supported by the course handout "Course Handout - ToC (Jan-June).pdf" and "Course Handout - ToC (Jan-June).pdf". The course "IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications" (CSE3722) has 3 credits (L-D-P: 3-0-0), with a final grade based on the marks obtained in the mid-term and end-term evaluations, and the major topics include IoT networks, architectures, and applications. This information is supported by the course handout "IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications-CSE 2023 Batch.pdf". The course "Discrete Mathematics" (CSE 3715) has 2 credits, with a final grade based on the marks obtained in various assessment components, and the major topics include discrete mathematics. This information is supported by the course handout "Batch 2023_Course Handout_Discrete Mathematics-Odd-2025-26.pdf" and "Batch 2023_Course Handout_Discrete Mathematics-Odd-2025-26.pdf". The course "Network Security" (CSE3714) has 3 credits (3-0-0), with a final grade based on the marks obtained in the internal assessment evaluations and the end-term assessment, and the major topics include network security. This information is supported by the course handout "Network Security (1).pdf".
